title: Restore fail-closed post-merge release source verification
rawUserRequest: 发布遇到流程或过时代码问题时先修复流程/代码，再继续 v0.5.39；用户批准方案 A，并授权 agent 判断、执行和监控 GitHub Actions。

declaredIntent.summary: Restart the abandoned unmerged correction from latest main, preserve candidate-content materialization, restore eager controlled-identity validation, and require independent review before immutable archive creation.

intent.problem: PR #329 correctly separated candidate content from merged identity, but independent review after ai-finish found that premerge finalization no longer eagerly validated the controlled source identity. Because its Work Item was already immutable, #329 was closed unmerged. The correction must restart from latest main with one fresh Work Item, retain the valid topology regression, restore fail-closed identity validation, and move independent review before ai-finish.
constraints: Do not amend or import the abandoned v1 archive into this PR.
Do not create or move v0.5.39 until this Work Item is merged, closed, synchronized, and exact main passes post-merge checks.
Premerge canonical content must come from clean candidate HEAD, while the controlled source identity must still resolve eagerly and remain the hosted post-merge identity reference.
Identity, tree, archive, lifecycle, and ownership mismatches remain fail closed before release mutation.
Use one Work Item, one branch based on latest origin/main, and one replacement PR.
Run independent code review before ai-finish so required fixes remain inside mutable active evidence.
Archive growth remains warning-only; do not raise complexity limits.
intent.rationale: Candidate content and merged identity are separate facts, but both must be validated at the earliest stage where each is knowable. Restarting the unmerged PR preserves immutable-evidence rules and restores one-Work-Item ownership.

outOfScope: Creating or publishing the v0.5.39 tag or GitHub Release
Weakening exact-SHA, controlled-ref, archive, source-tree, lifecycle, or ownership validation
Changing release metadata values before the later release-preparation Work Item
Raising complexity thresholds or creating a budget-only Work Item

sources.reason: Current finalizer and the reviewed eager-identity regression.
Existing finalizer and detached release-preflight coverage.
Hosted exact-main resolution and pre-tag mutation boundary.
Canonical archive inclusion and export-ignore boundary.
One-Work-Item PR and archive lifecycle requirements.
Repository precedent requiring a fresh correction Work Item after archive.
Evidence of missing eager controlled-identity validation and weak plan-cleanup assertions.
Process-first correction, delegated decisions, and CI monitoring authority.

problemStatement: Make candidate-to-merge release verification both content-correct and identity-fail-closed, while preserving immutable Work Item ownership after abandoning the reviewed unmerged PR.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Premerge finalization hashes clean candidate HEAD while preserving the controlled default-branch identity reference.
An unresolved controlled source identity fails finalization before canonical hashing or metadata rewrite.
A real candidate, no-ff merge, and fresh detached checkout accept unchanged canonical content.
Included-content drift after candidate merge fails closed.
Compacted plans retain their Contract/Summary/manifest references and the known-wrong superseded plan remains deleted.

riskAssessment.reason: An incorrect candidate/identity boundary could publish evidence for the wrong source; the correction also exercises immutable archive recovery.

executionDecision.reason: The user delegated evidence-based process decisions; the reviewed defect, recovery path, scope, acceptance, and verification are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Prove invalid controlled identity fails before metadata rewrite.
Review the real Git topology rather than mocked commit names alone.
Do not publish or tag in this Work Item.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Preserve source-binding, review-order, and no-publication constraints through the replacement correction.

acceptance: Premerge sourceTree and archiveSha256 come from clean candidate HEAD while sourceCommit, tagTarget, and metadataCommit retain controlled identity references.
The controlled source identity resolves eagerly; an invalid ref returns failure before canonical hashing or any release metadata rewrite.
A real candidate commit to no-ff merge to fresh detached checkout passes post-merge preflight when canonical content is unchanged.
Included-content drift remains fail closed before tag or release mutation.
Plan-cleanup regression verifies each compacted plan's existing Contract, Summary, and manifest evidence and verifies the superseded incorrect plan is absent.
Lifecycle documentation distinguishes candidate content from merged identity and requires independent review before ai-finish.
The replacement PR contains exactly this v2 Work Item from latest origin/main; PR #329 has state CLOSED and no v1 archive path appears in the replacement diff.
Archive growth remains warning-only and Markdown/Python budgets pass without threshold increases.
All local gates, independent review, replacement PR Actions, merge, ai-close-work-item, exact-main post-merge audit, and subsequent governed release preparation complete before v0.5.39 publication.
guidelines: Fail closed on release identity, lifecycle, source-tree, archive, or ownership mismatch.
Keep candidate content evidence distinct from provider-bound published identity evidence.
Preserve immutable archived evidence and use a fresh Work Item after post-archive correction.
Do not publish or tag until post-merge verification is complete.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user approved scheme A and delegated future evidence-based release-process decisions.

rollbackNote: Close the replacement PR without merge and preserve main; do not amend prior immutable evidence or retain stale origin/main hashing.

budgetImpact.reason: One replacement functional archive; the abandoned v1 archive is not part of main or this PR.
